title: Understanding RACI Chart Roles
summary: A concise overview of the four RACI roles—Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed—and how they structure responsibility and communication in project management.
content: R – Responsible: person who performs the work. A – Accountable: person who makes final decisions and signs off. C – Consulted: people whose input is needed before work proceeds. I – Informed: people who need to be kept aware of progress. The RACI matrix aligns expectations and reduces miscommunication.
